title: Grassy Snap-fresh Ukrainian Tagliatelle Pasta
description: A hangover-cure dish featuring tagliatelle, katsuramuki-cut, fodmap-friendly and served in a corn-husk wrap. The grassy notes of the Ukrainian dill and fresh parsley create a refreshing taste experience.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the tagliatelle according to the package instructions.
In a large skillet, heat the rapeseed oil over medium heat and add the minced garlic. Cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the mixed mushrooms to the skillet and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the Ukrainian dill and fresh parsley to the skillet and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes until wilted.
Add the cooked tagliatelle to the skillet and toss with the mushroom and herb mixture.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
In a separate pan, cook the fodmap-friendly sausage until browned, then chop into bite-sized pieces.
Warm the corn-husk wraps by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
To assemble the dish, place a portion of the tagliatelle mixture onto the center of a corn-husk wrap, followed by a few pieces of the cooked sausage.
Top with fresh dill and parsley, and fold the bottom half of the wrap up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the wrap into a tight cylinder.
Repeat with the remaining ingredients and serve immediately.
Garnish with additional fresh herbs, if desired.
